reviews.review_description: Absolutely LOVE this game!!! I tried taping the controllers to my legs but that didn't really work however I downloaded the app from the playstore on my phone for tracking and it works perfectly! Just wish there were more songs that I enjoy but there's a few that I can do over and over. Would fully recommend this game IF your tracking works. Would pay to use try the free trial first if available.

reviews.review_title: great if it works and a fun workout
reviews.review_description:
Setup can be tricky depending on how you want to track your feet, but if it works, it's just so much fun and a good workout.

About the tracking methods I tried:
- Tracking with the mobile app was unusable bad for me and was the reason I quit the app the first time I tried it shortly after release. I just tried it again on my S25U - while it looks fine in the phone app, tracking ingame is not usable (tried front/back camera with different backgrounds and positions) -> most of the time in vr it thinks my feet are just on the floor and together

A few days ago a friend asked about the app and I gave it another try to test some stuff for her. This time I tried alternative tracking methods.
- First I taped the regular quest 3 controllers to my knees, which worked good enough to play and have fun as a proof of concept -> For regular usage you can buy official straps or come up with something better than tape :D
- I also have a pair of Pro Controllers and those work incredibly good! I don't even have to use straps, cause I can just keep them in place by putting them under my elastic shoe laces

Don't get me wrong, there are many things that could be better and to be fair: It's clearly stated to be in early access. I hope the phone app will get some love - especionally since you don't have ingame hand tracking, if you use the contollers to track the feet.

But the game itself... while it's simple and kinda basic and the polish is far away from beat saber or synth riders, it's just fun and it also already has a good working custom song integration that just works.
I will give the app a 4 star rating, the game already gave me some fun hours and quite some sweat and it only costs a few euros.

The idea is cool and exciting. But it is a very low-quality buggy game. It confuses my two legs. The games are made by other people so there's barely any smooth or on beat moves. A lot of them are hard-level. You can't even listen to a song preview until after you've downloaded it.

I wish the developers would've just worked a little more to make the whole thing better. DEFINITELY not worth $20. Not even $10.

I came into this app knowing the drama behind this project, but being a fan of DDR and Dancerush arcade games, I had to get it anyways. The concept intrigued me, especially the body tracking using the phone camera. How seamless can this work? Especially for the Quest 3.

It actually worked really well. The connection on the network between the phone and Quest 3 depended on several factors. 1. Are you using a new enough/advanced enough smart phone, and 2. Is your network stable. If your network isn’t stable, do you know how to troubleshoot it? 3. Is your environment lighted and decluttered enough? If you’ve got these three things down, then the game works well. Does it hit the notes perfectly? I wouldn’t say so 100% of the time, but it is very playable and very enjoyable.

Quest 3 and 3s controllers uses infrared to track, so they must stay in view of the headset cameras. This can’t be improved upon as long as Meta doesn’t give the green light for 3rd party accessories to improve upon this. So if you were looking for the kind of experience in the arcade while strapping the controllers to your leg/foot (best strapped right below the knees, face of the controller facing the knee, and with your arms completely out of the way), you won’t get a very good experience. If you’re not a stickler for hitting the notes to perfection though, just dancing away is fine with this method. 

I worked up quite a sweat, and it was quite fun. The cool part were the community songs/beat maps that you can download straight from your headset. No need to connect to the computer. The game itself is not very polished, but if I’m honest, I’m not looking at graphics when I’m looking to play these kinds of games. I’ll take anything I can get. Being VERY lenient to an unpolished game, the only thing I really have to complain about is not being able to sort or search by difficulty level. The community of songs is great (including licensed music), but you really don’t know what the difficulty rating is until you download it. Don’t worry, the game itself comes preinstalled with a number of music that has various difficulty. 

Will it get completely dropped one day? Perhaps it already has, like most ppl believe (including me). Or perhaps Meta will make updates to it that will make this game unplayable or unconnectable to body tracking on smart phones. Or the smart phone app will stop getting updates. Etc. The concept of this game is excellent anyways. I am surprised it had been put aside after 2 years of work into this. They could have pioneered a new market towards foot games, like Beat Saber did for musical hands/sabers and the many subsequent music and workout apps that followed suit. The potential for this would have been pretty limitless once a team can perfect a solution to body tracking (there are 3rd party trackers you can buy, but they’re pretty niche so it doesn’t work for many apps). The camera use on smart phones was a good idea to start. 

I have no regrets paying full price for this. But I might regret it when it stops working, if it does,one day.

reviews.review_title: The foot movement doesn’t really work.
reviews.review_description:
You need to use your phone to capture foot movement, this really doesn’t work. You can also tie the controllers around your leg…. But that was too crazy.
Waste of money. Also, you can’t automatically claim a refund for this game, so would definitely avoid this game.
I love it but the tracking is a bit bad but its probably because im on the quest3

It was terrible the whole bit is having the leg tracking and that didn't work it's like playing basketball in the driveway with two cousins and being asked to stream that on tik tok. I didn't add some download to my cellphone. I did strap the joy sticks to my legs and didn't work. The worst part is it poked fun when I did it stopped the game and told me I failed.
Just purchased it worked for me with phone it's a really great workout too love it's so much just Google some videos before you start your free trail on how to set it up
IF you can get the leg tracking app to work. It took me an hour to get it working. It seems to prefer that your phone/tablet be in portrait mode.
You need to download an app on your phone and even then the tracking is terrible save your money
Can't get leg tracking to do anything just sits and watches fron my phone but cant access it in the headset . Need answers or refund

Zero stars for this game! Could not get the game to work with the VR leg tracking app. Never got to play the actual game bc I “used it for more than 2 hours”. Two hours of trying to get the game to work and failing repeatedly does not fit the refund  spelled out in the policy. Zero stars to Meta 3 also for not honoring a refund on a game they sell but does not work. 👎

this game is the most awesome game out there!!!  this is what ive always wanted in a vr game!  you need full body tracking, obviously.  wish i could apply the camera technology to my fighting games, like art of combat!

im not sure what others are complaining about.  i wonder if they didnt get the setup quite right, and that's why they dont like it.  i had to spend a while (15 min?) getting my phone in the right place.  pro tip:  a tripod helps.

ok,  so the leg tracking isnt perfect perfect perfect.  lets blame that on meta, as im not sure why the heck they still cant offer it.  its not that expensive and id gladly pay up.  but dance dash came up with this awesome system meanwhile, to let us use phones to get the rest of the way there.

and side point:  direct dl of community maps -- AWESOME.  you dont see bsaber and synth doing that!!!!!

keep up the great work guys!  ill leave a 5* on the android app too!!

Full body is shaky but it will work till I figure out how to strap my controllers to my feet. 
I got it on sale for a dollar,and in my opinion it’s worth five dollars 

Note: don’t buy at full price. Wait for it to go on sale

To play this game you either need straps for your feet, or use the app to set up your camera for full body tracking.
The app worked for me for about a day and it was really fun! (It’s not perfect but it was a good workout) But for whatever reason the IP address changed three times (that’s how you set up the tracking on your phone to the headset). I made sure to match the two from headset to app and followed all of the instructions given but the headset wouldn’t connect. I restarted the headset, the app, swapped WIFI, uninstalled then reinstalled both game and app. Nothing worked. So yeah, take caution. It might just die on you for no reason. Get it on sale if you can.

Edit: it started working again the next day but the tracking is significantly worse. If you want a rage game, here’s your pick.

reviews.review_title: Great, but need more hit songs!
reviews.review_description:
It works great for me, but really need to upgrade the song list… and where’s black pink ?? Plus like jennie is like 30secs long - just the chorus. 
😭😭😭

Kinda wish I paid more attention before buying.. game only works if you somehow tie the hand controllers to your feet.. which seems ridiculous or if you use your phone to download a secondary app that will then Record you... and send that tracking information somehow without Delay into your headset game... I might look for a refund option..

I generally don’t write reviews. But, I like this game and want to put in my thoughts.

I was very skeptical about this at first, not going to lie. I originally used their app to track my movements. It was a bit fidgety and didn’t track properly; and also caused my phone to overheat and battery to drop pretty quickly. I still enjoyed the workout regardless. 

I eventually got the straps; and I will say, it is an improvement (compared to the app). While it did count my steps more accurately, the calibration is off. Some examples: 
1. One shoe/foot being higher than the other
2. One shoe/foot would be turned or rotated, even when you’re standing straight forward
3. Shoe/foot just floating across the screen mid-dance. 

Aside from the above issues, I still enjoy the game. It has potential to be even better. Hopefully devs will take each feedback into consideration.

Total rip-off. Had my kid crying after trying to tie controllers to his feet and realizing he had just been scammed out of $20. He learned a lesson about researching before buying a game so worth it in the end I guess.  Stay away from this.

reviews.review_title: Well developed but has tracking issues
reviews.review_description: It’s kind of fun and has a great amount of songs to play but it has tracking issues so you have to be kind of looking down at your feet the whole time and if you don’t it tends to lose track and the feet don’t move for like 5 seconds after you look at them. The oculus have little cameras to track so that would obviously make it hard to track if they are at your feet. The developers did a really good job and I think you can have fun with this game but just know it’s not perfect because of the way the oculus’s are designed and the game is also still in progress.  So is it worth buying? I would say it depends, if you have a lot of money and don’t mind spending on some games then yeah definitely try it.  But if that isn’t you, then I would recommend getting  the straps on amazon and use the free trial of the game and if you don’t like it then just return the straps and don’t buy the game. Once every few months I hop on the game if I don’t really want to play anything else but that’s all. It’s really well designed and developed though it’s just the devices tracking that makes it hard.

reviews.review_title: Love the idea but it needs work
reviews.review_description:
The concept of the game is great, Why dance only with your hands? Let's get the legs involved.
Unfortunately there is no native leg tracking for the quest at the moment. The developers idea of using a phone app is great but it's not perfect. I got the tracking working good enough to be playable but the precision you want for a rhythm game is not there. I hope meta or somebody comes up with a cheap easy solution for great leg tracking soon but until then this game can never reach it's full potential.

reviews.review_title: While in BETA: The shoe straps work
reviews.review_description:
2025-01-17 while still in beta: 
The shoe straps work great as long as you've got them TIGHT. Buy the straps FIRST and get the game that way!
Lots of COMMUNITY song maps available. 
Finally found something potentially as tiring as Thrill of the Fight!
I use the app to get leg tracking, it’s not the best I’ve seen but it’s not the worst, 5 stars

Because the game is still in development, I'm bumping up my rating to support the devs even though it has some flaws which will hopefully be ironed out prior to full release. But I am super excited about the concept as a lifelong DDR fanatic. Super unique game and I was able to get the tracking to work with the phone app pretty quickly. In just 20 minutes I worked up a sweat. Excited to see where the game goes!
It was pretty fun, but I wish it had different environments like beat Saber, and it also needs custom colors, and it also needs multiplayer plus the tracking issues

reviews.review_title: kinda iffy on the tracking but great fun
reviews.review_description: used quest 3 and an iphone se 2020. worked WAY better then i initally thought, although i did have to recalbrate my shoes alot.. might of been because i kept moving positions due to dancing. not a huge amount of custom songs but a good amount. probably about 200 total. i downloaded the steam editor to make some of my own as well. pretty fun i got on sale for 4 bucks 100% worth it. at 20 bucks, maybe wait for a sale? alot of fun though! and its cool how i can now take my headset and my phone anywhere and play this. its like beat saber but with my shoes and hands now not just hands.

Was able to get the app set up without issue on my iPad. The only thing that would have made that experience better would be being able to find my Quest 3 instead of typing in the IP. 

I understand the game is early access but it is reeaalllyyyyy choppy on the Quest 3. Video stuttering, tearing, sometimes weird glitchy stuff just outside my field of view or above my head. Not just in song - it's jittering and freezing even just navigating the menu. I assume this will be much more polished in the future.

It can be hard to stay in the correct spot as you naturally move around, causing you to miss. Sometimes I seem to miss for no reason.

I wish the custom song downloader would allow you to hear a preview of the song so I can verify it's definitely the correct song you're thinking of, and not a different song with the same name.

Overall it's still fun and gets you moving, and I look forward to the more professional level of polish and smoothness that I'd experience with other games like Beat Saber and Synth Riders as it moves out of early access.

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving the game a try and sharing your feedback! We’ve made updates in the latest version to improve performance and reduce stuttering and shoe jittering when pairing with the Dance Dash mobile app. We’re continuing to polish the experience and hope you’ll give the new version a try. We’d love to hear your thoughts as we continue to improve!

reviews.review_description: The game itself is nice and the concept of using feet is great. I unfortunately don't own any other hardware than Quest 2 with controllers, so I have to rely on the app. And simply put, it works only sometimes. Legs tend to swap for no reason and I didn't find any fix. I tried other phone, but that didn't even send the video output right for some reason and the tracking acted like it doesn't exist. Also obvious lack of songs (it's early access, so I understand that fully). A good concept, it just needs to be polished. And the app needs to be fixed. Maybe redone completely.
This game is only fun if you’re able to track your movements with the mobile app, and even then it’s pretty buggy. Still fun though, got it for cheap since it was on sale

reviews.review_title: Lots of promise, but needs polish
reviews.review_description:
I really hesitate to give this 3 stars because I wanted this to be a 5 star product. I saw a Youtuber play it and was instantly sold on the concept, but unfortunately the execution (at least for the Meta 3S) isn't ready for mass consumption.

The setup wasn't too difficult, but the app-based foot tracking is too jittery to accurately reflect what my body is doing, which IMO is a requirement for VR rhythm games. I know the Youtubers I watched were playing on PCVR with hardware leg + arm tracking, which definitely helps.

The actual onboarding process is pretty weak right now too, with it thrusting you into downloading custom songs as soon as you finish the very basic tutorial. Admittedly, it is very nice to be able to download songs without having to jump through hoops, but for new user this is really intimidating.

I don't think I'll be spending much time with this game for now, but I do look forward to additional development and polish. Hopefully next time I fire it up, it'll be in a better state.
can't play the game because the app literally will not work with my phone, it's not even a super old phone either

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you're unable to install the app. Sometimes, Google Play may block certain device models, and unfortunately, this is beyond our control. As an alternative, we recommend taking a screenshot of the in-game app download guide. You can scan the QR code on the screenshot to access our download page. From there, please select the Alternative Source option to download and install the APK directly. If you encounter any issues during the process, feel free to reach out. We're here to help!

reviews.review_title: Doesn’t work don’t waste your time
reviews.review_description: I bought this game on 3 different devices pc, quest 2, and quest 3. And I haven’t been able to play the game. It doesn’t work on any of them. My shoes are below the floor which is very interesting and if I want hand and foot tracking I only get my hands so u can’t play

developer_response.body: My apologies for any negative experience you may have had with our game's leg tracking settings, which require special attention. The previous UI lacked proper prompts. We have just updated to version 0.5.5, which includes new UI elements that strengthen guidance for controller settings and BodyLink APP configuration. If you are still interested in our game, please give this new version a try and let us know your valuable feedback!

reviews.review_description: I am delighted and excited to see where this will go!  Just purchased and played on easy. This is audio trip for your legs! Once you use the shoe alignment, remember use your controller by your thighs. I received 90/98 accuracy. I'm basically a vr newbie and can't wait for this and audio trip to combine! Not that it was suggested lol just vr dreaming:)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support! We’ll keep polishing the game! As you only left us 3 stars can you let us know what areas we should improve so you love it? As well we have a free app which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset!

reviews.review_description: Horrible looking game with terrible performance. Tracking app is horrible and delayed. Cant use Quest 3 controller. Pro controllers don't even work. Should be removed form the app store.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry to hear about your experience and truly value your feedback. The latest update includes tons of performance and visual improvements, along with a detailed setup guides and tutorial to ensure Quest 3 and Pro controllers work seamlessly with Dance Dash. As well we have a free app which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset! We’d love for you to give it another try and share your thoughts if you’re still interested. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: To play you must have foot straps or an iPhone to track your body. Wish I would have known that before I bought it. Did not get to try it.

developer_response.body: I apologize for the inconvenience you experienced with using a controller for the game. Our game currently supports free mobile apps for leg tracking. If you are still interested in our game, please try our latest version 0.5.5 and share your valuable feedback with us!

reviews.review_description:
I think I would grow to love this game. I initially tested the game on my iPad, just to see if I will like it, and I did. I was looking into buying the strap before I purchase for my Quest 2 but I read somewhere that I can use the iPad App as sensor so I can still play without straps, that the camera of the iPad will be used for hand and feet sensor. That convinced me to buy at full price, but luckily when I checked the product description, there’s a coupon code that brought the cost down to $10 so I happily purchased. Played it yesterday and am very happy with my purchase despite me looking silly (am not a dancer).

I can’t judge if there are plenty of songs because I did not like how the filtering works. I would have preferred if there’s a quick way to show all jpop songs and etc.

After the latest update, it made it possible to use bodylink from ios  to motion accuracy. This is a great update. In addition to not spending money  expensive accessories.

It's fun to play, all day. It's no different than going to arcade  game.

reviews.review_title: Up there with beat saber for most fun
reviews.review_description:
I havent had this much fun in a vr game since first playing beat saber. 
Im using a quest 3 with the bodylink phone app and it works enough for me to do well in expert level tracks with hand and feet tracking.

The inbuilt community song downloader is brilliant as well.  Beat saber could learn from that.

Putting the controllers around your knees does not work well at all since they face your legs and do not fully face the headset cameras. They are janky and jittery and the shoes fly all over the place. The shoes are also a foot above the floor and tilted or angled in a weird way. If you click Adjust shoes, the two black bars that you're supposed to put into the shoes to show your feet position don't work. You move the black bar into a shoe and the shoe is now at your knee height. If you try to move the black bar to the floor, the shoes go underground. It's just not possible to do this without an incredible amount of frustration. If you try a song, while moving your feet, the in-game shoe stutters and drifts out of the target line even if you are not moving and have no body or hair  obstructing the controllers. 

They either need to release a strap for the knees with a plastic insert that protrudes way out from your body while pointing upwards at the headset camera and have a preset for that in the game so you don't have to manually set that up. Or, they need to make that same sort of strap that sticks out in front of your real shoes. That would be much better than the knee strap. I tried putting foam blocks behind the strap to push the controllers further away from me and there was improvement but not enough to call the game playable with these awful controllers that Meta ruined. 

If you have a Q2 or Pro controllers it may be a better experience, but it's unplayable on a Q3. 

Other than that: there should also be an option to turn off the walls in the game. If not off entirely, then the songs on Easy mode should not have walls. Let players get used to the footwork first before throwing other stuff at them.

Tried turning the hand targets on in the game and when I did that, the shoes flew up to my face.

The game is a mess on the Q3. Don't do it.

developer_response.body:
Sorry for your bad experiences with Quest3 tracking.
We have updated the game with BodyLink which uses phone app to track the legs, thus playable without straps. Hope it can help you enjoy the game!

reviews.review_description: This is a 5 star game.  Better than beat Saber.  A better workout too.  But the Quest version's framerates  isn't as smooth as it needs to be.  That's about the only weakness.  Works fine on Quest 2 just holding the controllers in your hands.
It’s not that fun when you don’t have gravity shows and I want my money back.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry to hear about your experience. Over the past few months, we’ve made significant improvements to enhance the gameplay experience in Dance Dash including adding a free android and iOS mobile app which performs full body tracking and sends this data back to your Quest headset! We’d love for you to give it another try and let us know what you think

reviews.review_description:
This is the only dancing game where your footwork is measured. Buying the accessories on Amazon for around $12 (which works for both the quest 2 and 3 ) is well worth it , and now you have a unique dancing game.

If you’re still on the fence, there are various discount code sites that you can google, such as metadog. Or just visit zzzzz🔘bio🔘link
Bought the straps and all. Major tracking issues with the Quest 2. Going to try for another day but so far very disappointing.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, and we’re sorry for the issues. Please ensure no obstructions between your controllers and headset. You can also try our free Dance Dash mobile app for leg tracking, which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset! Our latest update includes a setup tutorial which makes things much easier to use. Let us know if you need help!

reviews.review_title: Tracking has been trash for me
reviews.review_description: I love the look and game play I just can not get it to track well at all. I have the straps for it and my feet just go everywhere. Anyone else have this issue??? I'm on Quest 2.

developer_response.body: Thank you for enjoying the look and gameplay! For tracking issues, please ensure there’s no obstruction between your controllers and headset. You can also try using our free Dance Dash mobile app for leg tracking which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset! The latest update includes a guide on how to download and set it up. Let us know if you need further assistance!

reviews.review_description: Dance Dash is super fun and a great workout. Before leaving a bad review, just know that the game is still in alpha for Quest 3. I attempted to play on Quest 3 and tracking was so bad that it wasn’t playable at all. However, if you own Quest Pro controllers, it works great on Quest 3. For now, stick to playing on Quest 2 or on Quest 3 with Pro controllers.

I, like many others, am the type of person who likes to jump right into things without the use of instructions.  

That said, it took me a minute to realize that in order to get “bodylink” to work properly with my Quest 2 and iphone, I have to DELETE the IP address that automatically shows in the text box of the bodylink feature on the Dance Dash app on my iphone, locate the IP address on my quest, and type it into the app.  

  The instructions are clear as day on the wiki link provided in the headset, but why does that seem like the only place any info on this game is located?  

Once I figured that out,  i was having a jolly good time! Im absolutely floored that I can play this without the use of controllers, and that my feet could be tracked as well!

Im still in the “getting-a-feel-for-it” stages, but first impression is that it has the potential to be my new favorite game.  It reminds me of new school DDR.  

  I removed one star, due to;

  The lack of social media presence. No facebook? No insta? Only discord? I want to compete with people, but haven’t figured out how yet.  I randomly saw somebody playing this game on a tiktok. I screenshot an image of it, and did an image search, which is the only reason I discovered the game even though its been out for ab a year already!??  Why are the instructions on wiki? I wish Dance Dash was more intuitive and that I didn’t have to comb through so much text to figure things out.  A hands on tutorial would make things way more enjoyable.

  I saw that you can make an avatar, and dance videos with said avatar, but figuring this out is a maze as well.

  Lastly, I wish they had various stages to choose from, instead of the same stage for each song.
Only a bit glitchy...still yeah :/

Quest 2 here. Sorry but game is close to a trash. Tracking at feet/legs doesn't even work. It's often impossible to adjust them and if it somehow worked tracking is lost after one/two moves with your feet.
The only way to play it is with mobile app but... 
1. Camera 0 (at the back of phone) is reversing left and right feet.
2. It's tracking only left-right. I mean it is recognizing jumping i think? But moving your feet slightly up and down does nothing (at least most of time, it's hard to observe it with strong focus while dancing). The same with forward/backward.
3. Because of mobile app it's a latency so you'll be missing objects.
4. It's still losing tracks so very often your "shoes" will be combined even if you are standing normal with space between them.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback, and we’re sorry for the frustration. The latest update has improved the Dance Dash mobile app’s precision, reduced latency, and enhanced jump detection. We’re also continuing to optimize tracking stability. We hope you’ll give the new version a try and share your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: I’ll update my rating as I play more.   Foot straps work well. Navigate menus with your eyes done well.  There are like 15 songs to start. I’m gonna need a mat or something because I find myself moving forward without realizing it.  Played first song on easy normal and hard.  So far very fun and good workout, but I’ve only played one song 3 times.  Ps. Want to add I’m on quest 2. Tracking seems good so far but I need more time in game

reviews.review_title: Hand tracking no go but fun!
reviews.review_description: I love this game! Although the hand tracking does not work on the meta 2, I put no fail so I can still include that part when playing. Overall very fun and a good cardio game

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words! For hand tracking, it works with the app tracking feature where we have a free mobile app which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset! Please follow the in-game instructions to set it up. We’d love to hear your thoughts after trying it out! As you only left us 3 stars can you let us know what areas we should improve so you love it?

reviews.review_description: Bring your dance moves! 💃

I bought the game and it was complicated. I didn’t even have these certain straps that you need to play the game. I wasted $19.99 on a game that I needed to buy additional straps for, which are $29.99. The only thing that was fun was the songs. I tried to do them but they were just too complicated.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, you can play the game without extra equipment by using our free Dance Dash mobile app for tracking which can send Full Body Tracking data from your iOS or Android device back to the headset! Additionally, we’ve added tutorial songs in the settings, designed to help you start with simpler steps to learn at your own pace. We hope you’ll give it another try and enjoy the experience!

reviews.review_description: This is a lot of fun. Have been looking forward to it coming out, and yes, it is still in Alpha, so it is a little glitchy. The tracking got way off at one point, so I wasn't doing so well in the song. However, one or two little cool things I've found is that 1. You can strap the controller around your ankle instead of the top of your foot, much more comfortable, in my opinion. Just hit the controller adjust, and it will readjust the controller (rocket boots) to correct height and position. 2. There is hand tracking, so you can do both hands and feet in the songs. I don't have the hand trackers for that. But it's nice to know. Otherwise, it's a really fun game.

Love this so much. Has been what I’ve been looking for, but somehow isn’t out there yet?? This is so great, and of course I am so hopeful they keep developing it. I very much, more than I can possibly express, NEED for there to be a game that allows dancing to a Spotify playlist and auto generates the dance. In the meantime, this is so great, thank you!
